embeds.title: Morimens Covenants In A Nutshell
embeds.description:
In this video im explaining the Morimens Covenant System so Beginners can have a better understanding of it, together we are going over how to get covenants, how to upgrade them and how to start off as an early game player. Hope you enjoy!

-----------------------------------------------------------

I've seen a lot of E3 recs on guides
Takes on average, about 42 Pulls to obtain a Character on Rate Up
And there are also Proto Horizons which can be purchased in the Shop for 500 Radix Sediments once a Month
Which gives you a Dupe for a Character that you already own
42 pulls to get the banner character?
yeah i just knew that. quite great

Every 30 Pulls guarantee an SSR Character, 90 (or every 3rd SSR) guarantees the Rate Up

how about getting a limited on standard?
Rate can be shown in the Banner when you check Rules
Not viable targeting them there, but it is possible
Missing it on them also counts towards getting either of the Rare Ups
wait so u get them after a certain number of loses?

There's also currently a strat where you pull on Standard Realm Banners and aim to AA (E3+12 / 16 total Copies) of a Character to then use a Pyramid (Origin of Time, obtained once each in Half Anni & Anni) then transfer the Dupes into another Character with AA, as obtaining Dupes for AA Characters give you 1 Fragment for a Character Selector
Yeah, same as with Limited Rate Ups
Every 3 SSR Pulls will guarantee any 3 of the Characters shown on the Standard Realm Banner picture
you dont get limiteds in standard after a certain number
May have explained it pretty badly

Half Anni / Anni will have a special Banner called "Kaleidoscope smth" which will have each currently released Character's Banners rotate per Day
But it will not be transferred to Limited Character / Weapon Banner
Since it joins both the Character & Weapon together
You can pull Characters from the same Realm, as well as any Weapon
Get 2 of the same Character / Weapon, next is guaranteed of the other
This is for the Rate Up btw
